title: Repair false-close semantics for failed post-wave gates
description: Final review blocker: ticks are closed and branches cleaned before the post-wave integration gate. Rework sequencing so post-wave failure cannot leave known-bad work closed without repair state. Either delay closes/cleanup until the wave gate passes or reopen/retain repair branches atomically; recovery must block dependents on failed wave evidence.
acceptance_criteria: A failed post-wave test leaves every affected tick open/repairable with useful branch/worktree state retained, no dependent launches, and recovery treats the failed wave as blocking. Tests no longer codify closed bad ticks.
closed_reason: Completed: wave integrations are now provisional until post-wave tests pass; failed/incomplete gates retain open ticks, branches/worktrees, durable transaction journals and blocking recovery evidence; successful closes/cleanup occur only after gate evidence.
